title: zephyranthes albicans
intro: Zephyranthes albicans, also known as the white zephyr lily or white rain lily, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America, and is known for its white, funnel-shaped flowers that bloom after rainfall. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, and may also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
